description: Make `hex sched queue list` include running tasks (and any other non-terminal states) by default, with --all for terminal states and --status / --json for filtering. Closes the disagreement between `queue list` and `status`. Includes brain→sched title rename on this surface.

tasks.title: Refactor queue_list handler in hex-cli/src/commands/sched.rs to load all non-terminal tasks (status in {running, pending, failed_retrying}) by default, sort by status (running first) then created_at ASC. Add --all, --status <STATUS>, --limit (default 50), --json flags via clap.

Update table renderer: add STATUS column with ▶ running, ⤵ pending glyphs (matching `hex sched status` output). Title 'Pending Brain Tasks' → 'Sched Queue'. Footer line: 'N running, M pending, K completed (use --all to show)'.
Test: snapshot queue with 1 running + 2 pending + 1 completed; default render shows 3 rows (no completed); --all shows 4; --status running shows 1; --json output round-trips through serde.

evidence: hex sched queue list 2>&1 | grep -qE 'Sched Queue|running'
tasks.title: Workspace build + release rebuild + smoke: cargo check --workspace, cargo build -p hex-cli --release, then `hex sched queue list` shows current running task if one exists.
